industry.headline: Zelio E-Mobility Launches 60,000 Units/Year EV Plant in Coimbatore
industry.summary: Zelio E-Mobility opened a 39,000 sq. ft. EV manufacturing facility in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, with an annual production capacity of 60,000 units. The plant will handle electric scooter assembly, storage, logistics, and support operations across Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Kerala, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and South Maharashtra.

tech.headline: Triple-junction perovskite solar cell hits 27.3% efficiency
tech.summary: An international research team developed a triple-junction all-perovskite solar cell using a graphene oxide (GO)/self-assembled monolayer (SAM) bilayer contact strategy, achieving 27.3% efficiency. The innovation addresses charge extraction losses and improves interface quality in perovskite-based photovoltaics.

tech.headline: Australia's First Compressed CO2 Battery Project Begins
tech.summary: The Victorian government and Italian company Energy Dome plan Australia’s first compressed CO2 battery storage facility in Victoria’s coal region. The project aims to develop long-duration energy storage using CO2 compression technology. No specific capacity or timeline is mentioned, but it represents a novel approach to energy storage in Australia’s traditional energy sector.

tech.headline: Avangrid Begins Oregon Trail Solar Panel Installation
tech.summary: Avangrid, an Iberdrola Group company, started panel installation at the 57-MW Oregon Trail Solar project in Gilliam County, Oregon. The milestone supports 200 local union jobs and marks progress in the project’s construction. The solar farm is part of Avangrid’s expansion of renewable energy infrastructure in the US. The project is expected to contribute to regional clean energy goals.

tech.headline: Peak Energy to build 4-GWh sodium-ion ESS plant in Sacramento

learn.title: Electric Buses & Trucks: Powering Clean Transport
learn.intro: Electric buses and trucks cut emissions, reduce fuel costs, and support India's net-zero goals. Electrifying commercial transport is key to decarbonizing logistics and urban mobility.
learn.c1_text: Electric trucks emit 50-70% less CO2 than diesel. A 2023 study showed switching 100 trucks reduces emissions by 2,000 tons annually. Ideal for freight corridors and urban delivery networks.
learn.c2_text: Modern lithium-ion batteries offer 400-500 km range. Fast charging (15 mins for 80%) minimizes downtime. India's electric buses use 300 kWh batteries, charging in 2-3 hours.
learn.c3_text: India plans 40,000 EV charging stations by 2025. States like Tamil Nadu offer subsidies for electric buses. The FAME II scheme supports 10,000 electric buses by 2025.
learn.stat3: EV charging cost: ₹6-8 per km vs ₹15+ for diesel

learn.title: Solar PV: Power from Sunlight 🌞
learn.intro: Solar PV panels convert sunlight into electricity using the photoelectric effect. India’s solar capacity has surged to 70+ GW, powering millions of homes and businesses.
learn.c1_text: Photons strike silicon cells, knocking electrons loose to create current. A 1 m² panel generates ~150W under sunlight. No moving parts—pure physics in action.
learn.c2_text: Panels convert 15-22% of sunlight to electricity. A 5 kW system in India produces 6,500 kWh/year—enough for 7-8 homes. Efficiency varies with angle, temperature, and dust.
learn.c3_text: India added 12 GW solar in 2023, reaching 72 GW total. The PM Surya Ghar scheme aims to install 40 GW rooftop solar by 2026. Solar now powers 10% of India’s electricity.

learn.title: India’s ISTS Waiver: Cutting Renewable Costs 📉
learn.intro: India’s ISTS waiver policy lets states sell excess renewable power without inter-state transmission charges, boosting investments and reducing costs.
learn.c1_text: The 2019 waiver removed 12.5% IST charges for inter-state renewable sales. States like Gujarat and Tamil Nadu now export surplus solar/wind power freely.
learn.c2_text: Waiver reduced transmission costs by ₹1.2 trillion (2023). Developers save ₹3-5 per kWh, making projects viable. Solar tariffs fell to ₹2.44/kWh in 2023.
learn.c3_text: Waiver accelerated 100 GW+ renewable target. Karnataka exports 1.5 GW solar to neighboring states. Tamil Nadu’s wind farms supply power to 5 states.
learn.stat1: India: ₹1.2 trillion saved via ISTS waiver (2023)

learn.title: Battery Management Systems: Battery Guardians 🧠
learn.intro: BMS monitors and manages battery health, preventing overcharging/overheating. Critical for EVs and grid storage, ensuring safety and longevity.
learn.c1_text: BMS balances charge across cells. A 100 kWh EV battery with 98% balance lasts 10% longer. Tesla’s BMS uses AI to optimize cell performance.
learn.c2_text: BMS maintains 20-40°C range. Overheating reduces battery life by 20%. Liquid cooling in EVs like BYD’s Han improves thermal management.
learn.c3_text: Indian startups like Amprius develop BMS for EVs. Tata Motors uses BMS to extend battery life by 30%. NITI Aayog targets 100% BMS adoption in EVs by 2025.
learn.stat1: EV battery life: 8-10 years with BMS

learn.title: Hydrogen Colors: Green, Blue, Grey 🌈
learn.intro: Hydrogen’s color code reveals its production method. Green (renewable), blue (with carbon capture), and grey (fossil fuels) determine its environmental impact.
learn.c1_text: Made via electrolysis using solar/wind power. Emits zero CO2. India aims for 5 GW green hydrogen by 2030, targeting industries like steel and fertilizers.
learn.c2_text: Produced from natural gas with carbon capture. Emits 90% less CO2 than grey hydrogen. Saudi Arabia’s 2025 blue hydrogen plant will capture 1.2 million tons CO2/year.
learn.c3_text: India’s National Green Hydrogen Mission targets 25 GW by 2030. NTPC’s 1 GW green hydrogen plant in Gujarat uses 100% renewable power. Policy support includes ₹15/kg subsidy.

learn.title: Small Wind Turbines: Home Energy Solutions 🌬️
learn.intro: Small wind turbines (under 100 kW) generate power for homes and communities. They reduce grid dependence and are ideal for rural areas with consistent wind.
learn.c1_text: Vertical axis turbines (VAWT) are quieter and safer for homes. A 10 kW VAWT in a 6 m/s wind generates ~10,000 kWh/year—enough for 2 homes.
learn.c2_text: Turbines need 10+ ft clearance from obstacles. Wind speed of 12+ ft/s (6.5 m/s) is ideal. A 5 kW turbine in Rajasthan produces 7,000 kWh/year.
learn.c3_text: India’s small wind capacity is 110 MW. Tamil Nadu leads with 80 MW. The government offers 30% subsidy for rural installations. Costs dropped 40% since 2015.
